subject: ap-calculus-bc
title: Rate & Accumulation (Gravel Processing)
stimulus: On a certain workday, the rate, in tons per hour, at which unprocessed gravel arrives at a gravel processing plant is modeled by $G(t) = 90 + 45\cos\!\left(\dfrac{t^2}{18}\right)$, where $t$ is measured in hours and $0 \leq t \leq 8$. At the beginning of the workday ($t = 0$), the plant has 500 tons of unprocessed gravel. During the hours of operation, $0 \leq t \leq 8$, the plant processes gravel at a constant rate of 100 tons per hour.

parts.prompt: Find $G'(5)$. Using correct units, interpret your answer in the context of the problem.

scoring_points.description: Correct interpretation with correct units (tons per hour per hour)

required_elements: At $t = 5$ hours, the rate at which gravel arrives is decreasing at approximately $24.588$ tons per hour per hour
Units: tons per hour per hour

parts.prompt: Find the total amount of unprocessed gravel that arrives at the plant during the hours of operation on this workday.

scoring_points.description: Correct integral setup for total gravel arriving

parts.prompt: Is the amount of unprocessed gravel at the plant increasing or decreasing at time $t = 5$ hours? Show the work that leads to your answer.

scoring_points.description: Correct evaluation of $G(5)$ and comparison to processing rate of 100
Correct conclusion that gravel is increasing because $G(5) > 100$

required_elements: Since $G(5) \approx 105.460 > 100$, the amount of unprocessed gravel is increasing at $t = 5$

parts.prompt: What is the maximum amount of unprocessed gravel at the plant during the hours of operation on this workday? Justify your answer.

scoring_points.description: Sets up $G(t) - 100 = 0$ to find critical points and identifies candidates
Correct amount of unprocessed gravel expression using accumulation
Correct maximum value with justification comparing all candidates

required_elements: The maximum amount of unprocessed gravel is approximately $621.737$ tons, occurring at $t \approx 5.118$ hours
Justification: $G(t) - 100$ changes from positive to negative at $t \approx 5.118$, confirming a maximum
